thought_beat_text: The wall ends and the darkness behind it isn't cover, it's just absence — no floor confirmed, no direction, only the fact of stepping off the set into whatever isn't the mission. He's been standing at this seam long enough that the crowd noise from the other side has started to feel like something he owes, and the honest ring of it is worse than the void because it means the escape isn't clean.
residue_summary: The backstage darkness offers nothing but the removal of obligation, and that temptation is itself a kind of accusation about how much the performance has cost.
image_hint: figure silhouetted at the edge of a lit stage, one foot angled toward unlit nothing
audio_hint: crowd murmur fading into low-frequency hum, silence pressing from one side
